steps.title: Set up vertex form using the maximum point
steps.content: The maximum height of 282.24 feet occurs at $t = 4.2$ seconds. This is the vertex of the parabola. We write the height function in vertex form: $$h(t) = a(t - 4.2)^2 + 282.24$$

steps.title: Find the coefficient using the initial height
steps.content: The object was launched from an initial height of 0 feet, so $h(0) = 0$. Substitute: $$0 = a(0 - 4.2)^2 + 282.24$$ $$0 = 17.64a + 282.24$$ $$a = -16$$ So the function is $h(t) = -16(t - 4.2)^2 + 282.24$.

steps.title: Solve for the time when height equals 250.88 feet
